criteria: Longform (avg video >= 8 min), 3-4 total uploads, 100K+ total views, last upload within 3 weeks. Ranked by popularity (blend of avg views per video, outlier score, and consistency).

rankedChannels.title: ScreenPuff

topVideo.title: When an audition changed TV forever

rankedChannels.whyWorking: Nostalgic classic-film/TV anchor topics dressed as 'how did this win?' reveals. Hook+payoff structure on universally recognised IP (Oscars, old TV). All 4 uploads clearing 48K — none flopped. High RPM movies category. Highest outlier + highest avg views of the cohort.

rankedChannels.title: WINTHLETIC

topVideo.title: Thierry Henry Was Right About Mbappe

rankedChannels.whyWorking: MOST CONSISTENT performer in cohort — every upload is 83K–282K, median 157K. Rides a single evergreen obsession (elite footballer psychology) with authority-figure framing ('Thierry Henry was right'). Faceless, repeatable analysis format, only 3K subs — pure browse-surfaced growth.

rankedChannels.title: Sergeant Mike

topVideo.title: What It's Like To Be In Every Elite US Special Ops Unit

rankedChannels.whyWorking: One massive first-video outlier (1.27M) drove monetization. 'What It's Like To Be In Every X' is a proven listicle-doc frame applied to military — huge search + browse appeal to Aussie 45-70 male demo. Low outlier score because subsequent uploads dropped — watch: does the algo re-surface?

rankedChannels.title: Levina
rankedChannels.niche: mythical/prehistoric creatures + 'I got a pet' fantasy format

topVideo.title: I Got Pet Sea Ancient Monsters

rankedChannels.whyWorking: Hybrid format — classic myths/cryptids niche crossed with 'I got a pet [X]' personal-stakes framing borrowed from kids/fantasy gaming channels. Fantastic niche-bend example: folklore + first-person narrative = kid-adjacent retention on big topics. RPM is weak because audience skews young.

rankedChannels.title: Kira

topVideo.title: Your Life at Every Level of Attractiveness (1-10)

rankedChannels.whyWorking: Cross-pollinates history category with pop-psychology curiosity hooks ('Your Life at Every Level of Attractiveness 1-10'). The breakout hook format translates to any demo. High RPM when monetization clicks in. Filed under history for algorithmic reach — a category-arbitrage play.

rankedChannels.title: The City Past

topVideo.title: Why Iran Never Collapsed Like Other Empires | 5,000 Years of History of Iran

rankedChannels.whyWorking: Sleep/longform history plays (avg 38 min, one 72-min). Ridiculous watch-time = high RPM ($5.26). Iran topic is geo-political arbitrage — taps current news interest while staying evergreen. Huge advertiser value for this kind of retention.

rankedChannels.title: Mecha Files
rankedChannels.whyWorking: Super-consistent — 27K to 161K across all 4. Exploits a deeply passionate fandom (Gundam) starved of high-effort faceless content. High quality, high outlier, low competition at this depth. RPM is weak (anime) but watch-time is insane.

rankedChannels.title: True History Legends

topVideo.title: The Battle of Teutoburg Forest (9 AD)

rankedChannels.whyWorking: One blowout hit on a canonical ancient-battle topic. Evergreen military-history demo = premium RPM ($4.50). Format is template-friendly ('The [Battle] — [Subtitle]'). Threat: only 1-of-4 hit, others are DOA. Must test another angle to sustain.

rankedChannels.title: Professor Mass

topVideo.title: Every Bodybuilding Scandal Explained in 9 Minutes

rankedChannels.whyWorking: Two of three uploads over 90K with <1K subs — pure algorithmic browse. 'Every [X] Explained In [Y] Minutes' is a proven listicle frame. Scandal angle = CTR goldmine. Low RPM ceiling (fitness ad rates) but high volume potential.

rankedChannels.title: Animated Military

topVideo.title: Why the U.S. Navy Cannot Clear the Mines in the Strait of Hormuz

rankedChannels.whyWorking: Only 11 days old with a 128K hit on active news topic (Strait of Hormuz). News-pegged + evergreen-framed military content — the hottest hybrid right now. Strong RPM potential ($4.50). This is a 'watch daily' channel — if next upload hits, it's a breakout.

patternSummary: Authority-figure hook ('Thierry Henry was right...', 'Why X never collapsed') outperforms pure curiosity hooks in this cohort.
Niche-bending is the through-line: history+psychology (Kira), myths+'got a pet' (Levina), military+news (Animated Military). Pure niches underperform pure hybrids.
Avg video length 9-17 min dominates — the sleep/longform (38+ min) outliers (The City Past) are a separate play with top RPM.
Military/special-ops and classic-movies are the two most advertiser-friendly niches in the top 10 — both for the Aussie 45-70 demo Claudio already targets.
None of these channels are over 25K subs — all are pure algorithmic browse surfaces, meaning format/packaging (not audience) is doing the work. Replicable.
